topic: Sharp uniform-in-DGP non-equivalence theorem between three leading double machine learning (DML) estimators for the average treatment effect under unconfoundedness: the original DML cross-fit estimator (Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins 2018, Econometrics Journal), the locally-robust orthogonal-score (LRO) estimator (Chernozhukov-Escanciano-Ichimura-Newey-Robins 2022, Econometrica), and the automatic debiased ML estimator (Chernozhukov-Newey-Singh 2022, Econometrica). All three target the same population functional E[Y(1)-Y(0)] and have first-order-equivalent influence functions when nuisance functions are estimated at the standard L^2 rate o(n^{-1/4}). The flagship question (axis b: non-equivalence frontier between three named published estimators): characterize the sharp boundary in nuisance-misspecification rate space r_g = r_g(n) for the outcome regression and r_p = r_p(n) for the propensity at which DML, LRO, and ADML have asymptotically equivalent variance vs. strictly different uniform-in-DGP variance lower bounds. The kernel claim is a closed-form spectral non-equivalence theorem: the three estimators achieve identical asymptotic variance bounds (and thus identical Wald confidence intervals at first order) if and only if r_g + r_p > rho_star, where rho_star is a sharp threshold function of the smallest eigenvalue of an explicit observable covariance kernel formed from the outcome residual and the propensity Riesz representer. When r_g + r_p <= rho_star, LRO and ADML strictly dominate DML by orthogonality, but LRO and ADML themselves diverge in variance lower bound proportional to the spectral gap, with ADML achieving the global semiparametric efficiency bound and LRO achieving only a local one. rho_star is a NEW mathematical object computable from observable second moments, not an existence/absence statement from prior work. Recovers the textbook L^2-rate equivalence (Chernozhukov et al 2018) at the boundary r_g+r_p=O(n^{-1/4}) and provides the first sharp triple non-equivalence boundary for DML estimators in closed form, separating the regime where DML, LRO, ADML give identical inference from the regime where the ordering DML < LRO < ADML is strict and quantifiable.

open_problems.open_problem: Does a strict DML-LRO-ADML ATE non-equivalence frontier follow from the exact published variance-report formulas, or do the standard population variance maps force uniform equivalence?

source_refs.claim: DML uses cross-fitted Neyman-orthogonal scores for treatment and structural parameters under product-rate nuisance conditions.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: LRO supplies locally robust semiparametric GMM moments and misspecification-robust standard errors.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: ADML gives automatic debiasing and robust standard errors for causal effects including average treatment effects.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.excerpt: population variance maps coincide at the truth with identical Riesz-direction derivatives
does not derive B_{m,n} or the expansion from the published DML empirical AIPW variance

open_problems.why_unsolved: The three named papers establish valid orthogonal, locally robust, or automatically debiased inference within their own frameworks, but they do not jointly classify whether their reported ATE variance objects are equivalent or separated uniformly over DGPs. The direct v1 CausalSmith run found that the written population variance maps coincide at the truth, so the live obstacle is to derive the comparison from exact published variance or software reporting rules, or prove a no-go theorem for the standard population maps.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient theorem that either derives distinct first-order studentized limits from the exact DML empirical AIPW variance, LRO sandwich variance, and ADML robust Riesz variance, or proves uniform equivalence for those standard reports.

open_problems.open_problem: What are the closed-form binary-ATE variance-score summands and Gateaux derivatives for DML, LRO, and ADML under one common normalization?

source_refs.claim: The DML paper specifies cross-fitted orthogonal scores but does not provide a three-way variance-score comparison with LRO and ADML.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: LRO is a general locally robust GMM construction, leaving ATE-specific moment/Jacobian/variance choices to be pinned down for comparisons.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: ADML debiases functionals through Riesz representations and robust standard errors rather than hand-coding every ATE variance contrast.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.excerpt: Pin the exact published variance-functional formulas for DML, LRO, and ADML before Stage 0
Write the LRO first-step-adjusted variance summand and ADML kappa_A robust-variance augmentation

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing papers define the ingredients needed for valid inference, but the prior reviews show that a spectral or projection frontier is not auditable until the three exact variance summands are written in the same binary-ATE notation. The v1 derivation failed because estimator-specific certificate matrices were assumed or named rather than derived from the published formulas.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Closed-form DML, LRO, and ADML binary-ATE variance summands under one normalization, their nuisance-direction Gateaux derivatives, and an observable finite-support matrix S_n with a plug-in consistency theorem.

open_problems.open_problem: Which ATE-specialized LRO moments, Jacobian normalizations, and weights share the same efficient influence function as DML and ADML, and which produce distinct first-order estimators?

source_refs.claim: The LRO paper gives a broad class of locally robust moments rather than an equivalence classification for ATE-specialized choices.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: DML ATE inference is built around a fixed Neyman-orthogonal score for the unconfoundedness estimand.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: ADML constructs the bias correction from the functional and learned Riesz representer, which can coincide with hand-derived efficient scores in regular cases.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.excerpt: Pin the LRO ATE specialization by an explicit Jacobian/projection matrix
the canonical gradient belongs to the parameter/model

open_problems.why_unsolved: LRO is deliberately general, while DML and ADML can be specialized to the same ATE functional. The recurring prior-proposal warning is that the efficient influence function belongs to the model and target, not to an estimator label; without an invariant EIF baseline, an alleged DML-LRO-ADML frontier can confuse moment choice, normalization, and estimator non-equivalence.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An equivalence classification theorem defining the ATE EIF once, expressing IF_DML - EIF, IF_LRO - EIF, and IF_ADML - EIF for explicit LRO normalizations, and giving finite-support counterexamples outside the equivalence class.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the sharp joint outcome-regression and propensity/Riesz perturbation region where DML, LRO, and ADML are commonly valid, estimator-specifically valid, first-order separated, or jointly invalid?

source_refs.claim: DML gives sufficient product-rate conditions for orthogonal-score inference, not a sharp three-estimator partition of nuisance perturbation space.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: Regularized Riesz results give uniform validity and weak double-sparsity robustness for regression functionals, including ATE-like examples.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.claim: Calibrated DML targets doubly robust asymptotic normality when either regression or Riesz nuisance is good and the other may converge arbitrarily slowly.
source_refs.author: van der Laan, Luedtke, Carone

source_refs.claim: Doubly robust DML inference can fail regularity when only one nuisance is consistently estimated.
source_refs.author: Dukes, Vansteelandt, Whitney

source_refs.excerpt: sharp joint nuisance-perturbation region in outcome-regression and propensity-score error space
the two worst-case mechanisms compete for the same response types

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature has sufficient product-rate, double-sparsity, and calibrated-inference results, but these are not a sharp partition comparing the three named ATE procedures under joint pseudo-truth, rate, overlap, and Riesz instability. The prior joint-misspecification proposal flags the same structural obstacle: separate one-nuisance envelopes can be too rectangular because compatible perturbations may compete for the same observed-law degrees of freedom.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp partition theorem over nuisance rate exponents, pseudo-truth bias, overlap/Riesz instability, and joint compatibility constraints, with finite-support lower-bound witnesses for strict estimator separation outside the common-validity region.

open_problems.open_problem: Under local weak-overlap sequences, can DML, LRO, and ADML point estimates remain equivalent while their studentized Wald statistics separate for a real named variance rule?

source_refs.claim: Regularized Riesz inference covers regular and nonregular functionals and highlights Riesz-representer instability for ATE-like parameters.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.claim: Weak overlap can destroy regular root-n estimation, and thresholded doubly robust t-statistics recover normal inference under stated rate costs.
source_refs.author: Dorn

source_refs.claim: Approximate residual balancing emphasizes that poor overlap makes ATE estimation sensitive to extrapolation and weighting choices.
source_refs.author: Athey, Imbens, Wager
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B

source_refs.excerpt: point estimates can still be equivalent after V_n-normalization
Define Pi_{m,n} from the actual first-order expansion of each named variance estimator

open_problems.why_unsolved: Weak overlap is a concrete route to variance instability because inverse propensity and Riesz terms can grow even when the ATE remains identified. The v1 weak-overlap studentization idea was not settled positively; it was refuted for the population maps as written, leaving the narrower question whether an implementation-level variance report, thresholding, regularization, or local overlap experiment yields a genuine studentization gap.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A local weak-overlap asymptotic experiment with exact estimator and variance-report definitions, proving point-equivalence plus either a strict studentized-law gap for a named rule or an impossibility theorem for standard DML/LRO/ADML reports.

literature_map:
Chernozhukov et al. (2018, The Econometrics Journal, ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ) is the baseline DML source: cross-fitting plus Neyman-orthogonal scores for treatment and structural parameters under nuisance-rate conditions.
Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, and Robins (2022, Econometrica, ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO) gives the broad locally robust semiparametric GMM construction.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, Econometrica, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML) develops automatic debiasing for causal and structural regression effects and robust standard errors.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, The Econometrics Journal, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR) gives regularized Riesz representers, nonasymptotic uniform validity, and weak double-sparsity robustness.
Newey (1994, Econometrica, Newey1994Econometrica) and Robins-Rotnitzky-Zhao (1994, JASA, RobinsRotnitzkyZhao1994JASA) are the older semiparametric variance and augmented-IPW anchors.
Athey, Imbens, and Wager (2018, JRSSB, AtheyImbensWager2018JRSSB) supplies a balancing-weight comparison point and overlap-sensitivity motivation for ATE inference.
Velez (2026 working paper, Velez2026DML) studies DML1/DML2 separation with growing cross-fitting folds, showing that fixed-K equivalence can hide second-order differences.
van der Laan, Luedtke, and Carone (2024/2025 arXiv, VanDerLaanLuedtkeCarone2024CDML) targets doubly robust asymptotic normality by calibrated DML when only one nuisance is good.
Dukes, Vansteelandt, and Whitney (2024, JMLR, DukesVansteelandtWhitney2024JMLR) shows doubly robust DML inference may fail regularity when only one nuisance is consistent.
Dorn (2025 working paper, Dorn2025WeakOverlapDR) studies how much weak overlap thresholded doubly robust t-statistics can handle.
Bruns-Smith, Dukes, Feller, and Ogburn (2024/2026, JRSSB/arXiv, BrunsSmithDukesFellerOgburn2024ABW) connects augmented balancing weights/AutoDML to linear regression equivalences.
Bruns-Smith (2026, arXiv, BrunsSmith2026RieszApproaches) compares direct Riesz estimation approaches and finds equivalence for ridge/RKHS settings but not necessarily for lasso or general ML classes.
The main tension is not lack of orthogonal-score theory; it is the absence of a sharp, common-normalization theorem comparing equivalence, variance equivalence, and studentized-law equivalence for DML, LRO, and ADML under the same ATE law.
prior_proposal_map:
Direct prior is downgraded eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1: Stage -1 accepted a weak-overlap/studentization frontier, but Stage 0.5 found the named population variance maps coincide at truth.
Best reusable positive artifact is the certified-report calculus: if B_m certificates and variance expansions are supplied, Delta^B_m gives an iff studentization frontier and a two-cell strict-separation witness.
Main v2 warning: do not reuse the v1 strict DML<LRO<ADML claim unless the exact published DML, LRO, and ADML variance formulas produce the B_m matrices.
Highest-signal reviewer requests are: pin exact variance summands, derive LRO/ADML binary-ATE formulas, add arbitrary J-cell finite-support diagnostics, and define the equivalence relation upfront.
Adjacent estimator-frontier template is eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1: compare named estimator IFs to one true efficient EIF using explicit Gamma_m gap matrices and a minimal worked witness.
Adjacent DR/IPW DID qid warns that target equality, population-functional equality, IF equality, and variance equality must not be conflated.
pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 contributes a joint-nuisance/non-rectangularity caution: separate nuisance envelopes can miss compatibility constraints.
The study-pipeline gaps supplied with this run were LATE/Manski/interval-regression oriented and did not add a DML/LRO/ADML-specific source after the G1-G5 filters.
